source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Disc 1 obtains all the kinetic energy when the system, consisting of two identical discs of mass $M$ and radius $R$ supported by three identical torsion bars with restoring torque $\tau=-k \theta$, with displacements $\theta_{1}, \theta_{2}$ from the equilibrium position and initial conditions $\theta_{1}(0)=0$, $\theta_{2}(0)=0$, $\dot{\theta}_{1}(0)=0$, $\dot{\theta}_{2}(0)=\Omega$, is considered. How long does it take for disc 1 to get all the kinetic energy? You may leave this in the form of an implicit function.

candidate_problem_record.raw_question_text: Disc 1 obtains all the kinetic energy when the system, consisting of two identical discs of mass $M$ and radius $R$ supported by three identical torsion bars with restoring torque $\tau=-k \theta$, with displacements $\theta_{1}, \theta_{2}$ from the equilibrium position and initial conditions $\theta_{1}(0)=0$, $\theta_{2}(0)=0$, $\dot{\theta}_{1}(0)=0$, $\dot{\theta}_{2}(0)=\Omega$, is considered. metadata.reasoning_chain: If $I$ is the moment if inertia of each disc, the Lagrangian of the system is $L=\frac{1}{2} I\left(\dot{\theta}_{1}^{2}+\dot{\theta}_{2}^{2}\right)-\frac{1}{2} k\left[\theta_{1}^{2}+\theta_{2}^{2}+\left(\theta_{1}-\theta_{2}\right)^{2}\right]$. The two Lagrange's equations are $I \ddot{\theta}_{1}+k\left(2 \theta_{1}-\theta_{2}\right)=0$, and $I \ddot{\theta}_{2}+k\left(2 \theta_{2}-\theta_{1}\right)=0$. These combine to give $I\left(\ddot{\theta}_{1}+\ddot{\theta}_{2}\right)+k\left(\theta_{1}+\theta_{2}\right)=0$, and $I\left(\ddot{\theta}_{1}-\ddot{\theta}_{2}\right)+3 k\left(\theta_{1}-\theta_{2}\right)=0$. The solutions are respectively $\theta_{1}+\theta_{2}=A_{+} \sin \left(\sqrt{\frac{k}{I}} t+\varphi_{+}\right)$, and $\theta_{1}-\theta_{2}=A_{-} \sin \left(\sqrt{\frac{3 k}{I}} t+\varphi_{-}\right)$. The initial conditions $\theta_{1}+\theta_{2}=0$, $\theta_{1}-\theta_{2}=0$ at $t=0$ give $\varphi_{+}=\varphi_{-}=0$. The conditions $\dot{\theta}_{1}+\dot{\theta}_{2}=\Omega$, $\dot{\theta}_{1}-\dot{\theta}_{2}=-\Omega$ at $t=0$ give $A_{+}=\Omega \sqrt{\frac{I}{k}}$, $A_{-}=-\Omega \sqrt{\frac{I}{3 k}}$. Hence $\theta_{2}=\frac{1}{2} \Omega\left[\sqrt{\frac{I}{k}} \sin \left(\sqrt{\frac{k}{I}} t\right)+\sqrt{\frac{I}{3 k}} \sin \left(\sqrt{\frac{3 k}{I}} t\right)\right]$, and $\dot{\theta}_{2}=\frac{1}{2} \Omega\left[\cos \left(\sqrt{\frac{k}{I}} t\right)+\cos \left(\sqrt{\frac{3 k}{I}} t\right)\right]$. Only when $\dot{\theta}_{2}=0$, i.e. after a time $t$ given by $\cos \left(\sqrt{\frac{k}{I}} t\right)=-\cos \left(\sqrt{\frac{3 k}{I}} t\right)$, will disc 1 get all the kinetic energy.
